summary.intent: Add archive/hide functionality for sessions, fix session navigation to open in-app tabs instead of Terminal.app windows, implement a proper spawn-session tab with embedded terminal + live session detail view, and visualize parent-child session relationships in the sessions list.
summary.outcome: All four goals were completed: archive/unarchive buttons with toggle view, sync button, in-app spawn tab combining EmbeddedTerminal + SessionDetail, parent-child relationships stored in SessionParent table and surfaced via clickable parent ID cells in session rows.

repo: prisma db push is broken for this project — the FTS5 virtual table (LogContentFts) causes Prisma to attempt DROP TABLE which SQLite FTS5 shadow tables cannot handle. All schema migrations must be applied directly via sqlite3 CLI.
ArchivedSession and SessionParent tables were added to schema.prisma for type safety but must be manually created in the DB via sqlite3 due to the FTS5 conflict.

Parent-child session relationships are encoded in shadow branch task checkpoints at .entire/metadata/<parentSessionId>/tasks/<toolUseId>/checkpoint.json, containing { session_id, tool_use_id, agent_id }. The linking key is parentToolUseId on the child's first progress LogEvent.
New sessions appear in the DB immediately via LogEvent (SessionStart hook), before the shadow branch indexer picks them up — GET /api/sessions was extended to surface LogEvent-only sessions.
The spawn tab uses a polling approach to discover the new session UUID: poll fetchSessions() for sessions with startedAt >= spawnedAt - 5s.
SessionDetail has its own EmbeddedTerminal; when embedded in SpawnSessionScreen a hideTerminal flag is passed via React Router location.state to suppress the duplicate terminal.
Tab state is persisted to localStorage under key gossamer-tabs-v1; spawn tabs are ephemeral and not persisted.

code.finding: indexAllShadowBranches was extended to call indexTaskRelationships(), which reads tasks/*/checkpoint.json files from each shadow branch and stores parent-child mappings in SessionParent via db.sessionParent.upsert()

GET /api/sessions now loads db.sessionParent.findMany() and populates parentSessionId + childSessionIds on each returned session object. Also filters ArchivedSession rows by default, accepts ?archived=1.
Added command?: string prop — on ws.onopen, sends { type: 'data', data: command + '\n' } after 300ms delay to let shell initialize before typing the command.
New file: polls fetchSessions every 2s after 1s delay to discover the spawned session UUID, then renders SessionDetail via MemoryRouter above EmbeddedTerminal with hideTerminal:true state.
spawn tab type added with shape { id, type:'spawn', cwd, command, title, spawnedAt:number }. openSpawnTab() creates unique id via spawn:${Date.now()}.

Added SessionParent model { childSessionId String @id, parentSessionId String, toolUseId String? } with @@index([parentSessionId]). Also added ArchivedSession model { sessionId String @id, archivedAt DateTime @default(now()) }.

workflow: When prisma db push is blocked by virtual tables, apply migrations directly via sqlite3 CLI and keep the model in schema.prisma for type safety — the two sources of truth are decoupled but the generated client remains typed.

Raw SQL in server startup (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS) is acceptable as a belt-and-suspenders safety net for tables that can't be pushed by Prisma, but actual read/write operations should use typed Prisma client methods.
Using MemoryRouter to embed a routed screen (SessionDetail) inside another screen avoids needing to refactor SessionDetail's routing assumptions.

friction: prisma db push fails on every schema change due to FTS5 virtual table conflict — requires manual sqlite3 CLI workaround each time a new table is added.
Frontend TypeScript had pre-existing errors in CheckpointDetail, Repos.tsx, and SessionDetail that obscured build output and required filtering to find new errors.

Parent-child session linking required extensive investigation across shadow branches, checkpoint files, LogEvent tables, and full.jsonl transcripts before the correct linking key (parentToolUseId) was identified.
The agent_id field in task checkpoint.json is a short hex ID, not a UUID — required an extra join through LogEvent.parentToolUseId to resolve to a full session UUID.
process.env.HOME usage in ActiveSessions.tsx is a pre-existing issue that surfaced in tsc output but was not introduced by these changes.
open_items: The ArchivedSession CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS raw SQL call still exists in startServer as a safety net — could be removed since the table is schema-managed and the DB has it.
SessionParent relationships only appear after a shadow branch sync cycle — new subagent sessions won't show parent linkage until the indexer runs and the task checkpoint file is written.

The sync endpoint (POST /api/sessions/sync) triggers the shadow indexer but doesn't wait for completion — the frontend may need a retry/polling strategy to see parent relationships immediately after sync.
LauncherScreen.tsx was deleted but the launcher tab type may still exist in TabsContext — should verify no stale references remain.
The spawn tab is not persisted to localStorage (intentionally ephemeral) but this means refreshing the app loses in-progress session tabs.
process.env.HOME in ActiveSessions.tsx needs to be replaced with a proper IPC call to get the user's home directory in the Electron renderer context.
